description: Universal ad copy rules. Apply to ALL clients in ALL industries. Client-specific rules live in clients/<client>/rules-client.json.

rules.pattern: (?i)\b(we'?ll wait|simple math(s)?|it'?s not that hard|obviously|surely you|you should know|as everyone knows|clearly you|it goes without saying|any idiot|no brainer|duh)\b
rules.message: Condescending language detected. Condescension is the #1 kill signal in ad review. Rewrite to speak alongside the reader, never above them.

rules.pattern: (?i)^(stop|add it up|do the math(s)?|think about|listen|wake up|imagine if you|consider this|ask yourself|face it|admit it|let that sink in|read that again)\b
rules.message: Imperative command detected at opening. Commands tell the audience what to do — use invitations instead. 'Here's what it looks like' > 'Add it up'.
Em-dashes or en-dashes found. They read cold and editorial in ad copy. Use full stops, commas, or colons instead.

rules.pattern: (?i)(our (website|site|app|page|platform) (doesn'?t|isn'?t|won'?t|can'?t)|we'?re not (great|good|the best) at|we know (it'?s|our|we)|sorry (about|for) (our|the)|bear with us|we'?re (still )?working on)
rules.message: Self-deprecating language detected. Never undermine the brand in ad copy — confidence builds trust.

rules.pattern: (?i)(every(thing)? (you('?ll)?|they('?ll)?) (ever )?need|all you('?ll)? ever need|guaranteed to|100% (guaranteed|certain|proven)|never (pay|worry|spend|think) again|eliminates? all|solves? every)
rules.message: Overclaiming detected. Be specific about what IS covered/included rather than making absolute promises. Specificity beats superlatives.

rules.pattern: (?i)\b(swipe (up|left|right)|watch (this|the)|tap (here|the|below|above)|click here|scroll down|link in bio|hit the button|press play|double tap|save this post)\b
rules.message: Format-specific language detected. Ad copy must work across all placements (feed, stories, reels, audience network). Remove references to specific interactions.
Primary text exceeds 500 characters. Meta truncates long primary text — keep it tight.

rules.pattern: (?i)\b(act now|don'?t miss out|limited time|offer ends|hurry|last chance|only \d+ left|ends (today|tonight|soon|tomorrow)|before (it'?s|they'?re) gone|running out|while (stocks|supplies) last|expires? (soon|today|tonight))\b
rules.message: False urgency detected. Manufactured scarcity erodes trust. If the offer is genuinely time-limited, state the actual date in the ad set, not the copy.

rules.pattern: (?i)^(stop\.|before you scroll|wait\b|hold on|don'?t scroll|you need to (see|hear|read|know) this|attention|breaking:?|urgent:?)
rules.message: Aggressive opening hook detected. Aggressive hooks consistently fail in review — they feel like clickbait. Lead with value or a genuine insight instead.
Primary text opens with a dollar amount. Lead with a benefit or value proposition first — the price should support the value, not lead the conversation.
